data.title: Copperhead Resources Inc. approved a Change of Business to focus on subsea critical mineral exploration as Deep Sea Minerals Corp. (CSE: CUH) (OTCPK: CUHRF) (FSE: X45), approved by majority shareholders and the Canadian Securities Exchange.
data.description: Vancouver, British Columbia - On January 20, 2026, Copperhead Resources Inc. confirmed its transition to Deep Sea Minerals Corp. (CSE: CUH) (OTCPK: CUHRF) (FSE: X45) following shareholder approval received after the January 19, 2026, announcement. The Company's new focus on subsea critical mineral exploration has been well received by the majority of shareholders, with the Name Change also being endorsed. Additionally, the Canadian Securities Exchange has granted its final approval for the Change of Business, marking a significant milestone for Deep Sea Minerals Corp.

data.title: Refined Energy Corp. to launch charity flow-through private placement for fundraising.
data.description: A recent report reveals that the global population has reached 7.9 billion people, with over 50% of the world residing in urban areas. The study also indicates that around 2.2 billion individuals are overweight or obese, while over 690 million suffer from hunger or undernourishment. Additionally, the research highlights that nearly 1.1 billion people lack access to clean drinking water, and around 2.3 billion do not have basic sanitation services. These statistics emphasize the ongoing challenges faced by a significant portion of the world's population in terms of health, nutrition, and basic living conditions.

data.title: Key Tronic Corp (Nasdaq: KTCC) will report Q2 fiscal 2026 results on Feb 3, 2026 after market close.
data.description: Spokane Valley, Washington - Key Tronic Corporation (Nasdaq: KTCC) disclosed its intention to release the second-quarter fiscal 2026 results on February 3, 2026, after the market closes.

data.title: Teck announces 2025 production & sales update, affirms outlook; gives annual production volumes & guidance for 2026-2028.
data.description: Teck Resources Limited (TSX: TECK.A and TECK.B, NYSE: TECK) based in Vancouver, British Columbia, shared key unaudited data today. In the fourth quarter of 2025, the company reported its production and sales volumes, along with favorable settlement pricing adjustments. They also disclosed the annual production volumes for 2025. Looking ahead, Teck reaffirmed the production and unit cost guidance provided earlier for the years 2026 to 2028 for sites under its operation.

data.title: OpenAI uses behavior signals to restrict teen access on ChatGPT, detecting accounts likely belonging to minors for content limits, despite concerns of errors and bias.
data.description: OpenAI has integrated behavioral signals to detect accounts potentially owned by individuals under 18 years old. This technology enables automatic application of content restrictions. However, concerns have been raised by experts regarding potential inaccuracies and biases in this system.

data.title: Insilico Medicine and Hygtia Therapeutics collaborate on ISM8969 for CNS diseases, sharing 50% rights globally.
data.description: Insilico Medicine and Hygtia Therapeutics, a start-up supported by the Shenzhen Pengfu Fund of Fosun Health Capital and Fosun Pharma, have recently formed an exclusive partnership to further develop ISM8969 on a global scale. This collaboration involves a shared 50% ownership of worldwide rights between the two companies.

data.title: Dow Jones falls as Trump's Greenland threats fuel US trade concerns; S&P 500, Nasdaq break key support. Nvidia, Tesla, Netflix slump.
data.description: In light of President Trump's remarks regarding Greenland and upcoming Davos speech, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq saw a drop, breaking crucial support levels. Market giants like Nvidia and Tesla experienced declines, with Netflix also falling towards the end of the session. The Dow Jones suffered a significant plunge as a response to Trump's remarks escalating concerns over U.S. trade issues. This was reported in Investor's Business Daily, as the market reacted to the potential implications of the President's statements, leading to negative movements in key indices and major technology stocks.

data.title: Asia Pacific tech services market slumps in Q4 on lower demand for cloud and managed services: ISG Index.
data.description: In the fourth quarter, the Asia Pacific technology services market experienced a significant decline as reported by the ISG Index. This downturn was primarily driven by reduced demand for cloud services and managed services. The market saw a noticeable drop in numbers, highlighting the challenges faced by technology service providers in the region.

data.title: Rakuten Medical expands academic access to IR700 dye through fee-only provision, enhancing publication freedom and opening up commercialization opportunities.
data.description: Rakuten Medical has developed a light-activatable IR700 dye which is a key component in the approved medication Akalux™. This dye has been clinically proven to be effective and safe, with hydrophilic properties and minimal toxicity. The IR700 dye is versatile and can be utilized in various therapeutic approaches. Widening access to IR700 dye in academic settings is predicted to boost open innovation and...

data.title: Amtrak holding public board meeting online on Jan. 28, 2026 to discuss financial and operational performance.
data.description: On January 28, 2026, the Amtrak Board of Directors will hold a meeting that will be open to the public through virtual means. Executives from Amtrak are set to present reports on the company's financial standing and operational achievements. The meeting will cover important updates regarding major aspects of Amtrak's operations.

data.title: ACRES Commercial Realty Corp. prices $1.0 billion CLO backed by commercial mortgage loans with $879.5 million Offered Notes.
data.description: In Uniondale, New York on January 20, 2026, ACRES Commercial Realty Corp. (NYSE: ACR) shared news about its new subsidiary, ACRES Commercial Realty 2026-FL4 Issuer, LLC, planning to release $879.5 million in non-recourse, floating-rate notes known as "Offered Notes."

data.title: Hyundai Chairman Chung Kisun discusses AI and energy at Davos, attends 2026 Annual Meeting for fourth year, talks expansion with Palantir CEO.
data.description: Chung, Chairman, successfully attended his fourth consecutive 2026 World Economic Forum Annual Meeting, focusing on AI-driven transformation and innovation in energy transition during significant sessions. He also engaged in a meeting with Alex Karp, Co-founder and CEO of Palantir, to deliberate on expanding their strategic partnership. The meeting took place in SEOUL, South Korea.
